etymology.description: semantic 手 (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 辟 (pì) -> used to mean thumb
etymology.interpretation: The character 擘 (bò, "thumb") is constructed with the semantic component 手 (shǒu, "hand"), indicating that it pertains to something related to the hand. The phonetic component 辟 (pì), helps convey the sound. The specific application to 'thumb' makes intuitive sense as the thumb is a prominent feature of the hand that can be distinguished from the other fingers.
